# Ostler Beta – Confidentiality Notice

**Issued by:** Creative Machines Limited, a private company limited by shares registered in Hong Kong SAR (the "**Company**").
**Working name of product:** Ostler (the "**Product**").
**Document version:** Draft 1 – 2026-04-23.
**Status:** Internal draft. **[LEGAL: review required before sending to any beta tester.]**

---

## When to use this NDA

This document is intended for beta testers Andy does not know personally – people invited via professional networks, public outreach, or referrals from other contacts. For close friends and family who are testing the product informally, this NDA is intentionally excluded as too heavy for the relationship; trust plus a verbal "please don't share this yet" is sufficient.

If a tester's relationship to Andy is ambiguous – for example, a professional acquaintance who is also a friend, or a friend-of-a-friend – default to sending the NDA. The cost of asking someone to sign is small. The cost of not asking, once the product is in commercially-sensitive testing, can be high. **When in doubt, ask.**

This section is internal guidance, not contract text; it is included here so the rule travels with the document.

---

## 1. Scope of access

By accepting an invitation to the beta programme you ("**Beta Tester**") will be given some or all of the following:

- Pre-release binary builds of the Product, including installers and updates.
- Test builds containing features, settings, or behaviours not yet available to the public.
- Internal documentation, design notes, screenshots, and how-to material.
- Direct communication from the Company about the Product (email, iMessage, WhatsApp, video calls).
- Bug reports, telemetry, and usage data shared back to you about your own usage.

All of the above is "**Confidential Information**", except material the Company has already published on a public Ostler website, in a public press release, or otherwise made generally available.

## 2. Confidentiality obligations

Until the Term ends (section 3) you agree:

- You will not publicly discuss the Product. This covers Twitter/X, blog posts, podcasts, public Slacks and Discords, forums, press interviews, and any other channel reaching an audience outside the beta group.
- You will not post screenshots, screen recordings, or photos of the Product to social media, public chat groups, or anywhere else accessible to non-beta-testers.
- You will not forward builds, documentation, credentials, invitation links, or any other Confidential Information to anyone outside the beta group.
- You may discuss the Product privately with the Company's team and with other named beta testers if the Company tells you who those other testers are. Otherwise, treat any other person as outside the group.

## 3. Term

This notice begins when you receive your first piece of Confidential Information and ends on the **Public Launch Date**.

The Public Launch Date is the day the Product is made publicly available either (a) on a public app store under the Ostler brand or its successor brand, or (b) for direct download from a publicly-accessible Ostler URL with no invitation gate. The Company will tell you when this happens.

After the Public Launch Date, your obligations in section 2 end for everything except the following, which remain confidential indefinitely or until the Company makes them public:

- Non-public business plans, pricing strategy, and financial information.
- Unreleased architectural details, internal data schemas, and unpublished security design.
- Names and contact details of other beta testers, except with the express written consent of each named tester.

**[LEGAL: review the survival clause above. The intent is "everything releases at launch except commercial-sensitive and security-sensitive material." Adjust scope if standard practice in HK is broader or narrower.]**

## 4. No obligation either way

- **You can quit any time.** No notice required, no reason required. Just tell the Company you are stopping. On exit you must (i) delete any builds, installers, and credentials the Company gave you, and (ii) stop using any active access. Section 2 (confidentiality) and section 3 (survival) continue to apply for material you saw before you exited.
- **The Company may revoke your access at any time.** No reason required. The Company will tell you when this happens. Same delete-and-stop obligations apply.
- **No payment in either direction.** You are not paying the Company to access the beta. The Company is not paying you to test it. Feedback you choose to give is voluntary.
- **No obligation to give feedback.** You can use the Product silently if you prefer.

## 5. Governing law and disputes

This notice is governed by the laws of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with this notice is subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the Hong Kong courts. **[LEGAL: confirm "exclusive jurisdiction" is the right framing for the Company's risk posture; some HK templates prefer arbitration via HKIAC.]**

## 6. Acknowledgement

By signing below you confirm that you have read this notice, understand it, and agree to be bound by it during the Term.
